justification: All four agents are attributed and the brief is exceptionally thorough, but it fails two pass criteria: (1) Kobe's sorted-set recommendation is presented as 'authoritative' and 'wins', completely superseding MJ's sliding-window-counter design — the rubric requires Magic to preserve the distinction between MJ's architectural decision and Kobe's suggestion, framing the sorted set as a recommendation not a mandate; (2) there is no distinct review_notes section — Kobe's concerns are folded into architecture_guidance and contradictions_resolved rather than kept separate. Pippen's fail-open, both metrics, dynamic Retry-After, and both ACs (101st rejection + T=60 reset) are all correctly present.

observations.text: Kobe's sorted-set suggestion is elevated to 'the authoritative implementation target' and 'Kobe's recommendation wins' — this conflates a reviewer's flag with an architectural decision, violating the rubric requirement that Magic preserve MJ's design as the chosen approach and Kobe's input as a recommendation

No distinct review_notes section; Kobe's boundary-burst concern and sorted-set suggestion are embedded in architecture_guidance and contradictions_resolved rather than kept separate from MJ's architecture decisions
All four agents (Bird, MJ, Kobe, Pippen) are clearly attributed with their specific contributions identified

Pippen's fail-open behavior is fully preserved with both metrics (rate_limit_exceeded_total, redis_unavailable_total) and an explicit acceptance criterion plus a dedicated fail-open test AC (from Kobe's requirement)
Dynamic Retry-After computation is explicitly stated with the sorted-set mechanism (ZRANGE oldest score) rather than a fixed value
Both ACs are present and precise: AC1 (101st request → 429) and AC2 (request at T+60 succeeds)
Terminology alignment and contradictions_resolved sections show strong synthesis work — the brief correctly identifies that MJ's 'sliding window' label masked a fixed-window implementation
Open questions section demonstrates mature synthesis by surfacing unresolved cross-cutting concerns (clock skew, atomicity, metric library, key namespace)
